summary: Up from 3.8 to 3.9. Credit where it's due — four real fixes landed: activity filter now uses role='radiogroup' with aria-checked (correct ARIA pattern — done), the 480px min-height was corrected to 280px (resolving the grow-on-shrink bug), tabpanel tabIndex=0 removed from both panels, and the greeting now uses new Date().getHours() instead of hardcoded PST. However, the date display STILL uses timeZone: 'America/Los_Angeles' — half the fix shipped. The duplicate tooltip CSS block (Luna #7) was NOT removed, so z-index 20 still overrides z-index 100 on hover/focus. DEFAULT_ROSTER is on its EIGHTH flag. React.memo is on its SIXTH. The close button on the card back got a proper tabIndex — but the info button on the front still has no tabIndex override, so it remains tabbable when the card is flipped. Progress is real but the easy wins keep getting skipped.

suggestions.title: DEFAULT_ROSTER, VIBE_QUOTES, VIBE_WHITEBOARD, DESKS still inline in Office.jsx — 8th flag
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 6–70: VIBE_QUOTES, DEFAULT_ROSTER, VIBE_WHITEBOARD, and DESKS are all pure static data objects sitting in the component file. ROLE_EMOJIS and ROLE_COLORS were correctly moved to utils/constants.js ages ago. This split has been flagged in EVERY SINGLE REVIEW since #17. There are now EIGHT review flags on this item. Move DEFAULT_ROSTER, VIBE_WHITEBOARD, DEFAULT_BLOBERTO, and DESKS to utils/constants.js; move VIBE_QUOTES to utils/quotes.js. Estimated effort: 20 minutes. This is now a P0.

suggestions.title: Duplicate tooltip CSS blocks — Luna #7 block still present, overriding z-index
suggestions.description: Office.css still contains TWO tooltip rule sets. Block 1 (around line 423): the correct implementation with z-index: 100, opacity transition, and bottom: calc(100% + 6px). Block 2 (bottom of file, labelled 'Luna #7'): sets bottom: 100% (no gap), z-index: 20 (overrides Block 1), and hardcoded fallback colours. Because Block 2 appears later in the cascade, it wins on hover/focus — tooltip sits flush against the character with a lower z-index. Delete Block 2 entirely. Block 1 already handles all three pseudo-selectors correctly. Two-minute fix.

suggestions.title: Date display still hardcoded to America/Los_Angeles — greeting fixed, date not
suggestions.description: App.jsx: The greeting was correctly fixed to use new Date().getHours() (thank you). But the date display on line ~617 still uses timeZone: 'America/Los_Angeles' in the toLocaleDateString call. A user in Berlin on Monday sees Sunday's date. Remove the timeZone option from the toLocaleDateString call. One line. The greeting fix proves the team knows how — just finish the job.

suggestions.title: Card flip: info button on front still tabbable when card is flipped — 6th flag
suggestions.description: App.jsx WorkerCard: The close button on the back now correctly has tabIndex={isFlipped ? 0 : -1}. But the info button on the front (line ~185) still has NO tabIndex override. When the card is flipped to the back face, the info button is invisible but still in the tab order. Fix: add tabIndex={isFlipped ? -1 : 0} to the info button. One attribute. This has been flagged since review #19.

suggestions.title: CharacterAvatar and Character components still lack React.memo — 6th flag
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: CharacterAvatar is a pure rendering function of its props. Character is likewise pure. Neither is wrapped in React.memo(). On every 30-second poll, ALL Character instances re-render even when their props are identical. Two one-liner wraps. Zero behavioral change. Free performance win. This has been flagged in reviews #19 through #23 — five consecutive times. It is now on its sixth.

suggestions.title: Vibe streak counter still has no aria-label — title added but incomplete
suggestions.description: App.jsx: The vibeStreak span now has a title attribute (good — tooltip on hover works). But it still has no aria-label, so screen readers announce 'x7' with zero context. Add aria-label={`Vibe streak: ${vibeStreak}`} to the span. One attribute. The title was a good step — finish it.

suggestions.title: Whiteboard text still breaks mid-word at 375px — 4th flag
suggestions.description: Office.css: .office-whiteboard at 5.5% wide with font-size: 0.42rem. At 375px viewport that's ~20px. '🏆 SHIP IT' renders as 'SH IP IT' across three lines. Character names are already hidden at 480px. Apply the same treatment to whiteboard text: either set font-size: 0 and use a ::before emoji-only replacement, or bump width to 7.5% at the 480px breakpoint. Flagged since review #21.

suggestions.title: vibeStreak increments on page refresh, not on poll cycle — mechanic is broken
suggestions.description: App.jsx: vibe streak reads from sessionStorage and increments in a teamVibe.key effect. Within a single session, it only grows when the vibe key changes then changes back. A user who refreshes 10 times gets streak x10. A user with a tab open for hours never sees it grow. If the goal is 'consecutive syncs at same vibe', increment in the syncFromGitHub callback. If the goal is 'consecutive sessions', document it. Current behavior is confusing.

suggestions.title: WindowElement uses hardcoded America/Los_Angeles timezone for sky color
suggestions.description: Office.jsx WindowElement: getHour() uses Intl.DateTimeFormat with timeZone: 'America/Los_Angeles'. The sky gradient shows California time regardless of where the viewer is. A user in Tokyo at 2 PM sees a daytime sky because it's morning in LA. This is inconsistent with the greeting fix in App.jsx which now correctly uses local time. Apply the same fix: new Date().getHours().
